---
title: "Unit 1.4 Starter"
format: html
---

Load libraries and load in data

```{python}
import numpy as np
import polars as pl
from lets_plot import *
LetsPlot.setup_html(isolated_frame=True)


url = "https://byui-cse.github.io/cse110-course/lesson11/life-expectancy.csv"
df = pl.read_csv(url)
```

REVIEW EXERCISE:

Can you get a dataset that includes 3 countries (pick the United States and two other of your favorites)?


```{python}

df
```


```{python}
three = (
    df.filter(pl.col('Entity').is_in(['United States', 'Japan', 'France']))
    .rename({'Life expectancy (years)': 'Life'})
)
three 
```


```{python}
p = (
    ggplot(data = three, mapping=aes(x='Year', y='Life', color='Entity'))
    + geom_line()
    + scale_x_continuous(format ='d')
    + labs(title='Life Expectancy Through the Years')
)


p
```


```{python}
(
    p + theme_grey()
    + theme(plot_title=element_text(color='blue', size=20, hjust=.5), legend_title=element_text(color='pink'))
)
```
